rulings.Case Title: CESAR ASCARRUNZ VS. HOWARD HSU et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Millie Luongs Notice Of Motion And Motion For Mandatory Dismissal Of Ascarrunzs Action For Failure To Bring To Trial In Five Years
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, November 10, 2014, Line 1, DEFENDANT MILLIE LUONG'S Motion For Mandatory Dismissal Of Ascarrunz's Action For Failure To Bring To Trial In Five Years. The motion is denied. The order dated June 30, 2014, does not apply to the cross-complaint. The underlying motion was only directed to plaintiff's complaint. CCP 583.310 does not mandate dismissal of the cross-complaint. The 5-year period is measured from the date of filing of that pleading. See Perez v. Grajales (2008) 169 Cal.App.4th 580, 590. Ms. Luong's argument that the complaint and cross-complaint are the same and/or there is an improper splitting of claims needs to be raised in a different motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Order Compelling Rresponses To Request For Production And Inspection Of Documents And Other Tangible Things And For An Order Imposing Monetary Santions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, November 10, 2014, Line 1, DEFENDANT SELIM SOLTANE Motion For Order Compelling Rresponses To Request For Production And Inspection Of Documents And Other Tangible Things And For An Order Imposing Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Peter Catalanotti, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: No courtesy copy of the moving papers was provided. Defendant Soltane is ordered to email a copy of the moving papers and a proposed order (in Word format) to pcc@manningllp.com by 5pm today. All parties to appear at the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: PROGRESSIVE DIRECT INSURANCE COMPANY VS. MARK S. DAVIS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Reclassify Case As A Limited Civil Case (Walker Motion)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, November 10, 2014, Line 6, DEFENDANTS MARK DAVIS, BUDGET RENT A CAR SYSTEM, INC.'S Motion To Reclassify Case As A Limited Civil Case (Walker Motion). The motion is denied. Assuming that the property damage claim has settled, which is not entirely clear, defendant still fails to demonstrate that plaintiff's claim will necessarily result in a verdict of less than $25,000. Defendant merely relies upon the $413 doctor's bill and the arbitration award. Plaintiff declares that his quadriceps have a "grotesque scar" and his finger is "grotesquely disfigured" and he is having follow up care with a hand specialist. Without further information, the court cannot find that plaintiff will be unable to obtain an award exceeding $25,000. The court denies the request for sanctions. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT

The court overrules defendant's dilatory demurrer. Plaintiff has standing to maintain this action as an assignee. Plaintiff need not plead details or evidentiary facts regarding the assignment to state a cause of action. The existence of the legal malpractice action does not defeat plaintiff's claims. The court suggests that the parties stipulate to an order consolidating the two cases. rulings.Case Title: ERIN JONES VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants City And County Of San Francisco, San Francisco Police Department, San Francisco County District Attorney'S Office, George Gascon And San Francisco Sheriff'S Department'S Demurrer To 2nd Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, November 10, 2014, Line 15, Defendants City And County Of San Francisco, San Francisco Police Department, San Francisco County District Attorney's Office, George Gascon And San Francisco Sheriff's Department's Demurrer To 2nd Amended Complaint. Demurrer sustained. As a threshold matter, Plaintiff has standing to bring this action under CCP section 526a. The Complaint alleges that she has paid taxes within the requisite timeframe and seeks to prevent the illegal expenditure of funds by officers and agents of the state, city, and county. (See Wirin v. Horrall (1948) 85 Cal.App.2d 497 [holding that the mere "expending [of] the time of the paid police officers of the city of Los Angeles in performing illegal and unauthorized acts" constituted an unlawful use of funds which could be enjoined under § 526a].) As to the first cause of action, Plaintiff fails to state a claim upon which relief may be granted. The procedures allegedly followed by Defendants in providing the notices of forfeiture proceedings conform to the requirements of Health & Safety Code section 11488.4. The procedures also comply with Cuevas v. Sup. Ct. (2013) 221 Cal.App.4th 1312, which mandates that either the Attorney General or the district attorney make the forfeiture decision, as the Complaint alleges that police officers consult with the district attorney concerning the facts of the matter, and obtain authorization prior to providing the notices. Thus, the decision to proceed with forfeiture is allegedly and legally made by the district attorney. Furthermore, neither the statute nor Cuevas require the district attorney to personally draft, sign, or deliver the notices. Moreover, Health & Safety Code section 11488.2, as applicable here, requires only that the seizing police officer either refer the matter in writing or return the property. The statute does not limit the authority of the district attorney to make the forfeiture decision based upon any such written referral. Finally, the Complaint does not allege that the form of the notices fails to conform to the statutory requirements. Regarding the notices provided prior to the change in practices, the Complaint fails to seek any remedy available to Plaintiff. As to the second cause of action against all Defendants and to all causes of action against Defendant Kamala Harris, the Demurrer is moot in light of Plaintiff's Request for Dismissal filed on August 27, 2014. Plaintiff has 10 days leave to amend. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: FEDERATED INDIANS OF GRATON RANCHERIA VS. KENWOOD INVESTMENTS NO. 2, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses From Plaintiff'S Responses To Defendant'S First Set Of Request For Production
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, November 10, 2014, Line 6, DEFENDANT KENWOOD INVESTMENTS NO. 2, LLC Motion To Compel Further Responses From Plaintiff's Responses To Defendant's First Set Of Request For Production. Pro Tem Judge Peter Catalanotti, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing Required. Both parties to email proposed orders (in Word format) to pcc@manningllp.com by 5pm today. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ROFF CARTER VS. ATOMIC SKI USA, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, November 10, 2014, Line 11, CROSS DEFENDANT MARK MACKAY'S MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD. Granted. No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: SBERLO FAMILY TRUST VS. TANYA PHILLIPS (3/19/12-AMENDED TO CONTRACT ACTION)
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Cross-Complaintant Tanya Phillips To Compel Responses To Supplemental Interrogatories And Demands For Production Of Documents To Cross-Defendants Noel And Nadine Sberlo

Pro Tem Judge Peter Catalanotti, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing Required. Both parties to email proposed orders (in Word format) to pcc@manningllp.com by 5pm today. =(302/JPT)
